response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong web and full-stack credentials, but he is a poor match for this embedded networking security role. He lacks the required native-level Japanese proficiency, has only brief professional C experience (roughly six months at National Instruments), and shows no evidence of Linux kernel modification, L2/L3 switch development, or security technologies such as MACsec or 802.1X. His career centers on web platforms in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than embedded firmware.
strengths: 18+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded exposure
Strong Linux background and low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, high-performance services at Dunnhumby)
Demonstrated engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching/workshops
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, a hard requirement for the role
Minimal professional C experience and no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification track record
No experience with switch/networking security domains (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP, chip-vendor SDKs)

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering seniority and Linux fluency
